summary: Iteration 20 — Full backend verification of Comp-Off leave deduction bug fix. Tested both deduction paths in approve_leave() and apply_leave() with grant-based and manual balance scenarios, plus CL/SL regression checks. All 29 tests passed.

test_results.A_approve_leave_grant_path: PASS (6/6) — created test comp_off_grant for RMF0010, inserted pending Comp-Off leave, admin approved via PUT /api/leaves/{id}/approve, grant confirmed status='used' + used_at set, balances/all shows remaining=0
test_results.B_approve_leave_manual_balance_path: PASS (4/4) — set manual Comp-Off balance (3 remaining) for RMF0016, inserted pending Comp-Off leave, admin approved, Comp-Off.remaining decremented to 2, Comp-Off.used incremented to 1
test_results.C_apply_leave_autoapprove_grant_path: PASS (5/5) — created test grant for RMF0012, HR applied Comp-Off on behalf (auto-approve triggered), grant confirmed 'used' immediately with used_at set
test_results.D_apply_leave_autoapprove_manual_balance: PASS (4/4) — set manual Comp-Off balance (4 total, 1 used, 3 remaining) for RMF0005, HR applied on behalf, Comp-Off.remaining decremented to 2, used incremented to 2
test_results.E_cl_leave_regression: PASS (4/4) — CL leave approval still correctly deducts CL balance after Comp-Off fix; approval_type='cl' (not hardcoded 'sl')
test_results.F_sl_leave_regression: PASS (4/4) — SL leave approval still correctly deducts SL balance after Comp-Off fix; approval_type='sl'
test_results.H_compoff_approve_no_grants_no_crash: PASS (2/2) — approving Comp-Off leave when employee has no grants and no manual balance returns 200 without error; for loop is a no-op

critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix is correct and complete: both approve_leave() (lines 849-874) and apply_leave() (lines 372-394) now properly handle Comp-Off deduction.
Deduction priority is correct: manual balance (leave_balances.Comp-Off) checked first, falls back to oldest approved comp_off_grants grant.
Edge case handled: if employee has no approved grants AND no manual balance, the for loop is a no-op and returns 200 (no crash).
approve_leave() returns approval_type='comp_off' in response — consistent with other leave types.
GET /api/leaves/balances/all correctly shows 0 remaining after the only approved grant is marked 'used' (grant-count fallback path works).

test_credentials.note: All tests used admin account only. Employee accounts not needed for backend testing.

seed_data_creation: Inserted 2 temporary comp_off_grants (for RMF0010 and RMF0012) during test setup; both deleted by cleanup fixture. Set manual Comp-Off balances for RMF0016 and RMF0005 during tests; cleaned up (unset) by fixture. All test leave applications cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Comp-Off leave deduction bug fix fully verified (iteration 20). Both code paths in approve_leave() and apply_leave() are working. Key confirmed behaviors: (1) grant-based deduction: oldest approved comp_off_grant marked 'used' with used_at set; (2) manual balance path: leave_balances.Comp-Off.remaining decremented, .used incremented; (3) deduction priority: manual balance > grants; (4) no crash when no grants/balance available; (5) CL and SL regression confirmed working. DB state is clean after tests. Only the pre-existing RMF0009 approved grant (id: 69fc6cf27746668d4e9276f9) remains.

compoff_not_deducted_on_approval.root_cause: BALANCE_TRACKED = ['CL', 'SL', 'EL', 'Marriage'] — 'Comp-Off' was missing. The code only ran $inc for leave types in this list. Comp-Off approval silently succeeded without any balance deduction.
compoff_not_deducted_on_approval.fix_applied: Added a separate `if new_status == 'approved' and deduct_type == 'Comp-Off':` block after the BALANCE_TRACKED check in both approve_leave() and apply_leave(). Deducts from manual leave_balances.Comp-Off first, falls back to marking oldest approved comp_off_grants record as 'used'.
compoff_not_deducted_on_approval.test_verification: All 6 deduction tests pass: grant path (A, C), manual balance path (B, D), regression CL (E), regression SL (F), no-grant idempotency (H).
